title: OOF empirical-Bayes strategy priors for high-cardinality IDs
summary: `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions are high-cardinality and likely carry stable stop-style signal, but naive target means leak badly.
rationale: `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions are high-cardinality and likely carry stable stop-style signal, but naive target means leak badly. Regularized target encoding literature and CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling both suggest shrinkage/ordered statistics can outperform crude frequency features on this kind of tabular data.
implementation_hint: Create out-of-fold smoothed encodings for `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, `Driver×Compound`, `Race×Compound`, and `Race×Stint`, with hierarchical fallback (`Driver×Compound` -> `Driver` -> global). Compare LightGBM/XGBoost on these features against plain frequency counts; if using CatBoost, test raw categoricals plus only the new numeric priors, not one-hot.
expected_effect: Better generalization on rare drivers/races and a lift over current freq-based categorical handling, especially on public LB where entity mix shifts.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race/year, the encodings will overstate CV. Gains may be small if CatBoost already absorbs most of this signal.
